transcript_spans.text: So you want to learn how to make a sticker. Us mountain bikers love our stickers and here at IFHT we make a lot of them. Not only do we stick them on everything we own but for the last four years we've made tens of thousands of stickers and shipped them to over 80 countries from Canada to Norfolk Island and to tell you the truth I don't even know where that is. To let you in on a little secret here's how we make them and how you can too.

Here's what you need. The main tool is the cutter. We use the Silhouette Cameo. And yeah, it's used by scrapbooking moms, but for 300 bucks, it does the job. Aside from that, you'll need vinyl, transfer tape, scissors, an X-Acto knife, and a computer with Silhouette Studio, which comes with the cutter.

A hand holds up a smartphone to take a photo of the handwritten sticky note. The scene then transitions to a laptop screen, where the photo is opened in Adobe Photoshop. transcript_spans.text: I also like to refine my designs in Photoshop, but it's not necessary. Alright, now let's make a sticker. Firstly, you'll want to make the design. This can be a simple image, text, or even handwriting. I'm going to make a dirt surfing sticker, because there's nothing better than feeling surfy on your bike on some brown pow. I'm going to choose my favorite design, take a photo of it, and place it in Photoshop. What you want is to clean the image up and make it black and white.

If you have a tablet, you can also design with that. Keep in mind, the cutter can't do very intricate designs and you'll be peeling the negative space of the design manually. So you'll want to keep it simple. Once you have a clean image, you can bring it into the Silhouette Studio software.

This software has a trace feature you'll need to use to make the outline. To save vinyl, I like to fill the full width of it. In this case, I can fit three stickers at three inches a piece. You can plug the computer directly to the cutter, or you can load the designs onto a USB stick and plug that in.
